summary: All three top-3 buy candidates (JEM 6.7, PRQR 6.67, TDIC 6.6) are verified dilution/pump traps that must be vetoed, while 4 of 5 held names carry live distribution/dilution overhangs (UUUU $300M ATM + fresh 7/9 insider Form 144, NTSK 1.75M-sh 7/8 Form 144 post-lockup, WRAP $200M standing baby-shelf, CAI data drift) -- no NEW offering hit EDGAR today, but the standing overhangs remain and the book is at 9/4 weekly buys (overtrading).

WRAP.note: $200M universal S-3 baby-shelf on file (last prospectus/S-3 activity Feb 2026) on a sub-$100M post-+48%-pump name = standing drip-dilution capacity. EDGAR verified: NO July offering/424B5; 7/13 8-K is a WrapShield product launch, not a raise. Position +$3.59 at $2.19. Cache price was stale (7/8 $1.555 vs broker) per audit -- broker is account of record.

UUUU.note: Active $300M ATM (equity distribution agreement 2026-06-13) is a live dilution mechanism; EDGAR verified NO July draw -- 7/14 8-K is only an investor presentation. New wrinkle: Form 144 (Chalmers, 7/9) is a proposed insider SALE, so the insider picture behind the score (Bhappu/Hansen buys) is now mixed. Lorentzian k-NN -0.75 bearish; position -$1.17.

CAI.note: Fundamentals clean (director $990k buy 7/13, Caris Detect launch, $100M buyback, no dilution). Kept a notch up: nightly audit flagged broker_disagrees (broker $17.50 vs cache $16.99, 3.0% drift, HELD) -- though broker_state now reads $16.98, so the drift appears to have converged intraday. Data issue, not fundamentals; reconciliation matches.

NTSK.note: Book's best gainer +$5.16 at $14.05, breakout catalyst (Wells Fargo/Jefferies/Cantor Buy) intact, but post-IPO-lockup VC distribution is ongoing and confirmed on EDGAR: Form 144 for 1.75M Class A shares listed for sale (7/8) atop the 7/9 Lightspeed XII sale. Iconiq's $7.2M 7/8 buy only partly offsets; net insider flow is distribution.

YEXT.note: No shelf/ATM/dilution filing; failed Feb-2026 take-private replaced by an anti-dilutive $140M self-tender buyback. Position +$0.95 at $5.51, no fresh negative catalyst.

flags.why: Top-3 buy candidate (6.67) but management just diluted ~30%: EDGAR-verified $50M underwritten registered direct (27,624,310 sh at $1.81) + $9.2M Lilly private placement priced 6/25, closed 6/26, plus a 7/6 F-3 registering a further 3,030,303 PIPE sh for resale. Stock ~$1.72 sits BELOW the $1.81 offer; the CEO's $1.59 7/7 buy that drives the score came AFTER the raise. Not the clean insider fundamental the 6.67 implies.

Top-of-radar candidate (6.7) but a 12-for-1 reverse split is effective TODAY (7/14, new CUSIP G8071C137) purely for Nasdaq $1 bid-price compliance (Rule 5550(a)(2)), shares/outstanding cut 8.06M->~672k, layered on an unfunded 'AI+blockchain+crypto/stablecoin' pivot pump (+130%/-39%/+27% whipsaw). Reverse-split-plus-crypto-pump + a corporate action that desyncs ledger vs broker; not a fundamental insider catalyst. FinBERT +0.919 is a misattributed generic-market headline.

Top-3 candidate (6.6) with a textbook pump-and-dump signature: HK event-management microcap ran ~$0.23 (6/12) to >$10 intraday (6/16) then collapsed to ~$3.58. Extreme two-way volatility, no fundamental driver; the insider-driven score should be rejected by the pump veto. Cache also stale per audit.

On watchlist framed on the Equinor $1B+ backlog, but a pending ~$5.8B all-stock acquisition of Valaris (15.235 RIG sh per Valaris sh) dilutes existing holders to ~53% of the combined company and now carries a DOJ 'second request' -- parties won't certify before 7/31, pushing earliest close to ~9/29. Material dilution + binary-vote risk the 'clean backlog' framing omits.

Watchlist candidate that priced a $100M underwritten offering (13,495,277 sh at $7.41) on 6/24 -- real dilution alongside the ABS-201 Phase 1 catalyst; ~$10.07 now sits above mean analyst PT. Keep flagged so it is not bought as a fresh breakout.

Distressed reverse-merger pop, not organic strength: definitive Serapha Bio merger plus $230M concurrent private placement leaves pre-merger holders only ~3.7% of the combined entity; oncology pipeline halted, ~75% workforce cut. Still on today's scan (2.06) with a -3.0 internal penalty.

Most recent 10-Q discloses going-concern doubt including possible Chapter 7 liquidation; just closed a $23.7M private placement (warrant overhang). Still surfaced on scan; FinBERT -0.288 on the cash-runway story.

Scan candidate (2.96, below threshold) with severe dilution overhang: ~167M shares out, active $300M S-3 shelf, 13.95M warrants, authorized shares hiked to 450M. Phase 2 news constructive but capital structure unresolved; desk avoid.
Automatic S-3ASR shelf effective 2026-01-13, already used twice via 424B5, plus an active $50M ATM -- ongoing drip-dilution setup. On today's scan (1.83) with a -1.5 internal penalty.

On today's scan (1.79) but distressed: down ~91% YoY near 52-wk lows despite a token insider buy, 10-for-1 reverse split this cycle tied to Nasdaq compliance, pivoting into Dogecoin mining hardware -- distress signal, not a turnaround.
Live $100M ATM (since 2024) plus a Feb-2026 $107.6M underwritten offering (20M sh at $5.00), now underwater near $3.73-3.83. Desk avoid list; not held, not a current scan candidate.

dilution_watch.note: $50M underwritten registered direct (27,624,310 sh at $1.81) + $9.2M Lilly private placement (5,100,780 sh); closed 6/26. 7/6 F-3 registers 3,030,303 PIPE sh for resale. EDGAR-confirmed; no NEW July offering.
$300M ATM equity distribution agreement, usable now. EDGAR verified NO July draw (7/14 8-K is investor presentation only); Form 144 (Chalmers, 7/9) is a proposed insider sale.
~$5.8B all-stock Valaris acquisition (15.235 RIG sh per Valaris sh); existing holders diluted to ~53% of combined co; DOJ second request pushes earliest close to ~9/29.
$100M underwritten offering, 13,495,277 sh at $7.41; immediate dilution.
Definitive Serapha Bio merger + $230M concurrent private placement; existing holders diluted to ~3.7% pro forma.
$300M shelf plus 13.95M warrants; authorized shares hiked to 450M -- severe overhang.
$23.7M private placement; going-concern disclosure in most recent 10-Q.
Automatic shelf effective, used twice via 424B5; $50M ATM active.
$200M universal baby-shelf on file for a sub-$100M post-pump name; EDGAR verified NO July 424B5 draw -- standing dilution capacity only.
12-for-1 reverse split effective today for Nasdaq $1 bid-price compliance -- corporate action that desyncs ledger vs broker; not a fundamental catalyst.
$107.6M underwritten offering now underwater; $100M ATM remains active.

disagreements: RIG: intel's 7/14 alert now DOES acknowledge the ~$5.8B Valaris all-stock deal + DOJ second request (progress from the prior 'clean backlog' framing), but still tags it 'info/watchlist'; I keep RIG an elevated DO-NOT-BUY given ~53% dilution and a binary shareholder-vote/antitrust event.

NTSK: intel reads net insider flow as 'mixed'; EDGAR shows a 7/8 Form 144 for 1.75M Class A shares listed for sale on top of the 7/9 Lightspeed sale -- the sell side is larger and ongoing, so 'mixed' understates a distribution overhang the +7.2% pop is masking.

Discipline (carried): radar.html execution checklist shows weekly buy budget 9/4 used -- the book is well over the 4-buy weekly cap; surface as a standalone overtrading flag independent of any single ticker.
Convergence note (not a conflict): intel's flags_for_bot now includes JEM, TDIC, WRAP and PRQR, matching my prior snapshot -- the earlier PRQR/WRAP disagreements are resolved.
